markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games' official delay of Grand Theft Auto VI to November 19, 2026, has extended the timeline and widened the window for major developments before launch. This second postponement from the prior May target prioritizes polish on the Vice City-set open-world title, aligning with Take-Two's pattern of safeguarding quality for its flagship franchise. The shift has reshaped the 2026 release calendar, prompting competitors to adjust dates around the anticipated cultural juggernaut. Traders are now weighing fast-moving catalysts like geopolitical resolutions, music drops, and AI milestones that could resolve before the game hits PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X|S, with market-implied odds reflecting real-time sentiment on these high-uncertainty outcomes.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent mistrials in Harvey Weinstein’s New York retrials, including the May 2026 deadlock on the remaining charge involving Jessica Mann, have left key allegations unresolved while his separate 16-year California conviction stands and faces appeal. At age 74 and described as medically fragile, Weinstein has been incarcerated since 2020, with traders viewing the consecutive nature of sentences and ongoing legal challenges as factors that could limit additional time served. The dominant “no prison time” consensus reflects expectations around successful appeals, health considerations, or case resolutions that avoid further lengthy incarceration, though the California term and any future proceedings remain swing factors ahead of potential sentencing or retrial developments.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The closely contested 2026 Stanley Cup Final, currently tied 1-1, underpins the near-even implied probabilities for the Carolina Hurricanes and Vegas Golden Knights. Carolina topped the Eastern Conference with 113 regular-season points and advanced through a dominant playoff run that includes a perfect 6-0 overtime record this postseason, while Vegas leveraged its 2023 championship core and comeback resilience to steal Game 1 on the road before falling in overtime in Game 2. Both clubs boast elite regular-season form, depth across forward lines, and strong goaltending, with the series now shifting to Las Vegas for the next two games where home-ice advantage and recent momentum swings could narrow the gap further. Trader consensus reflects the unpredictable nature of a best-of-seven matchup between two battle-tested Eastern and Western Conference champions.

Jalen Brunson's 13 fourth-quarter points helped the Knicks seize a 1-0 series lead and home-court advantage after entering as underdogs. The Spurs, paced by Victor Wembanyama and a young roster that reached the Finals with the second-lowest average age in league history, retain strong rebound potential at home in Game 2 and beyond. Traders reflect the wisdom of crowds in pricing both sides near 50 percent, underscoring a matchup where recent form, series momentum, and home/road dynamics leave little separation heading into the remainder of the best-of-seven contest.

markets.question: Will MegaETH perform an airdrop by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if MegaETH launches a token and performs an airdrop by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market "locked" tokens or non-swappable tokens will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Airdrops of NFTs will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market is on-chain information and official information from MegaETH, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.description: this is a market on MegaETH airdrop

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The tight clustering at the top of the 2026 World Cup winner market stems from several European sides entering the expanded tournament with comparable squad depth, recent Nations League results, and proven knockout-stage experience. Spain and France share the highest implied probabilities thanks to balanced attacking options, strong defensive structures, and minimal reported injury concerns ahead of the June 11 kickoff. England sits just behind on the back of consistent qualifying form and coaching stability, while Portugal, Argentina, and Brazil remain competitive through individual star power and historical pedigree despite varying recent momentum. The bunched pricing underscores how evenly matched these contenders appear on paper entering group stage play across North America.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping maintains firm control over the Chinese Communist Party, state institutions, and military as preparations advance for the 21st Party Congress in 2027. Recent leadership reshuffles and military purges have been viewed by observers as efforts to further centralize authority rather than indicators of vulnerability. No successor has been promoted to the Politburo Standing Committee, policy continuity was reaffirmed at the October 2025 Fourth Plenum, and reporting shows no confirmed internal challenges or health developments capable of forcing removal before the end of 2026. Traders price these structural and personnel factors as making any early exit highly improbable.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Democratic presidential nomination for 2028 features a wide-open field with no established frontrunner, producing dispersed trader consensus across dozens of candidates. Gavin Newsom’s leading position stems from his executive record as California governor and sustained national profile, while Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez draws support from progressive constituencies and Kamala Harris from prior visibility as the 2024 nominee. Differentiators among contenders include state-level governing experience, fundraising capacity, appeal to key voting blocs, and positioning on major policy issues. Consolidation around one outcome would likely require strong early-state polling, major endorsements, or standout performances in primary debates and caucuses during the 2027–2028 cycle.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

events.title: Democratic Presidential Nominee 2028

eventMetadata.context_description: The Democratic presidential nomination for 2028 features a wide-open field with no established frontrunner, producing dispersed trader consensus across dozens of candidates. Gavin Newsom’s leading position stems from his executive record as California governor and sustained national profile, while Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez draws support from progressive constituencies and Kamala Harris from prior visibility as the 2024 nominee. Differentiators among contenders include state-level governing experience, fundraising capacity, appeal to key voting blocs, and positioning on major policy issues. Consolidation around one outcome would likely require strong early-state polling, major endorsements, or standout performances in primary debates and caucuses during the 2027–2028 cycle.
